description: Septoria leaf spot is a fungal disease that primarily affects tomato foliage and can cause severe defoliation under warm, humid, and wet conditions.
symptoms: Spots typically have gray or tan centers with darker margins
Tiny black fruiting bodies may be visible in lesion centers
Symptoms usually begin on lower leaves
Severe infection causes yellowing and defoliation

favorable_conditions.additional_conditions: Warm humid conditions favor repeated infection cycles

immediate_actions: Remove heavily infected lower leaves where practical
biological_control: Use registered biological fungicides where locally available

chemical_control.recommended: Protective fungicides may be used where disease pressure warrants treatment.
chemical_control.warning: Follow local registration, product labels, and resistance-management recommendations.

severity_guidance.low: Few spots on lower leaves. Remove affected tissue where practical and monitor.
severity_guidance.moderate: Increasing lower-canopy infection. Improve cultural controls and consider registered treatment.
severity_guidance.high: Severe defoliation or rapid spread. Implement integrated disease management.

farmer_advice: Septoria often begins on lower leaves, so early scouting of the lower canopy is important.
disclaimer: Septoria can resemble bacterial spot and other fungal leaf diseases. Confirm uncertain diagnoses.
